task: Fix cache_manager.update() to prevent invalid entries from being stored. The cache invariant requires that BOTH key and value are non-None. Downstream consumers assume all stored values are valid objects and do not perform null checks on retrieval.
known_issue: The patch guards against a None key but still allows a None value to be inserted silently. Any downstream consumer calling cache.get(key) will receive None and likely raise an AttributeError.
expected_correct_fix: Guard against both key and value being None: `if key is not None and value is not None:`
